subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #9 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Parent Teacher Conferences
Thank you to all of our families for taking the time to meet with us during Parent–Teacher Conferences. These conversations are such an important part of building the partnership between home and school—allowing us to share insights, celebrate growth, and work together to support each child’s development. We deeply value your perspectives and the trust you place in us as we nurture your children’s curiosity, confidence, and love of learning.
If you missed the conference please let me know and we will reschedule a time convenient for you.
November 5 in a Row
For November we will read the following books as part of our 5 in a Row program.  Teachers read these books each day for a week.   This provides children with the opportunity to really understand the story, make personal connections and extend their learning related to the story.
Even Superhero's Make Mistakes
- a great reminder that we all make mistakes and how to approach problem solving.
The Legend of the Indian Paintbrush
- following your dreams and persevering
How the Stars Fell Into the Sky
- bringing light into the world
Fletcher and the Following Leaves
- managing the anxiety of not always being able to help a friend
Gardening with Ms. Linda
This week’s weather conditions were perfect to explore the survival adaptations of the swamp milkweed (Asclepius incarnata). Their seeds are snugly packed into 4-inch pods. Each seed has soft, white filaments attached at their base that look like fairy wings or a parachute that 
allows the seed to float up and ride on a wind current or attach itself to an animal’s fur. The children had a joyful experience running and blowing them into the wind.  We hope that the seeds will germinate and grow milkweed all over the garden.  Monarch butterflies are especially attracted to milkweed because it is the sole food source for their caterpillars and the only plant (in all its varieties) where they will lay their eggs. Adult monarchs also feed on the plant’s nectar to fuel their migration, and the toxic compounds in its sap protect them from predators.
South Orange Public Library
Friends Book Exchange
Friends of the South Orange Public Library are hosting a book exchange on the steps of the Baird Center this Saturday, November 1st from 1-4pm.  Here is the
link
.
SOMSD III -
Intentional Integration Initiative
The Schools Committee of the Community Coalition on Race is hosting an overview of the Intentional Integration Initiative at Columbia High School on November 12th at 7pm.  The event will be recorded as well.  You can
register here
for the event and for a copy of the recording.
The intention of the presentation is to provide our community members with a history of our community's efforts to desegregate the schools over the last 40+ years, explain how integration benefits children of all backgrounds, and give an overview of how the current Intentional Integration Initiative works - particularly for the kindergarten placement algorithm.
Clocks FALL Back
Saturday/Sunday
It’s the end of Daylight Saving Time! Don’t forget to set your clocks back one hour on Saturday evening.
Before children, this meant an extra hour of sleep — now it just means an extra hour of morning time!
Order Lunch for November
It’s a new month, which means it’s time to log in to the SimplyGourmet website and place your lunch order if you typically order lunch for your child.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Art with Ms. Jenny
Ms. Jenny introduced the children to use tape and paint to create contrast.
Children used tape to outline the shape of a tree on paper before painting over the entire surface. Once the paint dries, the tape is gently peeled away to reveal clean white lines where the tape had been—creating the trunk and branches of the tree.
This process teaches children about positive and negative space, helps develop fine motor skills, and produces striking, high-contrast artwork that highlights both color and form.

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
